_source: ASE Heavy Duty Truck certifications, Diesel engine manufacturer service manuals, Troubleshooting guides
_note: Long-tail diesel mechanics knowledge. Engine diagnostics, air systems, emissions (DPF/SCR/EGR), electrical troubleshooting, driveline.

four_stroke_cycle.intake: Piston down, intake valve open; draws in air only (no fuel yet); turbo boost pressure increases air mass
four_stroke_cycle.compression: Both valves closed, piston up; compression ratio 15:1 to 20:1 (higher than gas 10:1); air heated to 900-1000°F
four_stroke_cycle.power: Fuel injected near TDC (top dead center); auto-ignites from heat of compression (no spark plug); expansion forces piston down
four_stroke_cycle.exhaust: Piston up, exhaust valve open; pushes burnt gases out to exhaust manifold

fuel_injection_systems.mechanical_inline_pump: Bosch P7100; cam-driven plungers; mechanical governor; reliable, simple; pre-1994 mostly; no electronics; tunable
fuel_injection_systems.distributor_pump: Bosch VE, DB2, Stanadyne; single pumping element distributes to all cylinders; lighter, cheaper; moderate pressure <2000 PSI
fuel_injection_systems.heui_hydraulic: Navistar 7.3L/6.0L; uses engine oil pressure (3000 PSI) to pressurize fuel; injection pressure control (ICP) valve; oil quality critical
fuel_injection_systems.common_rail: Current standard; high-pressure pump (30,000+ PSI) supplies rail; injectors electronically controlled; precise timing and quantity; quieter; multiple injections per cycle
fuel_injection_systems.eui_eup: Electronic Unit Injector/Pump; Detroit Diesel Series 60, Caterpillar C15; cam-actuated injector; combines pump and injector; high pressure; precise control

turbocharging.function: Exhaust spins turbine, turbine spins compressor, compressor compresses intake air; more air + more fuel = more power
turbocharging.boost_pressure: Typical 15-30 PSI; intercooled (air-to-air or air-to-water) to reduce intake temp; cooler air = denser = more oxygen
turbocharging.wastegate: Controls max boost; diverts exhaust around turbine above set pressure; prevents overboosting and turbo damage
turbocharging.vgt_variable_geometry: Movable vanes in turbine housing; changes A/R ratio; quick spool-up, high top-end boost; electronic or pneumatic control; common on modern diesels
turbocharging.turbo_failures: Shaft play (bearing wear from lack of oil, dirty oil, lack of cool-down), compressor wheel damage (FOD, overspeeding), oil leaks (seal failure), exhaust leaks pre-turbo (loss of boost)

egr_exhaust_gas_recirculation.purpose: Reduces NOx emissions by lowering combustion temp; recirculates exhaust into intake
egr_exhaust_gas_recirculation.types: Cooled EGR (heat exchanger cools exhaust before intake); uncooled EGR (older)
egr_exhaust_gas_recirculation.egr_valve: Controls flow; vacuum or electronic; common failure (sticks open or closed from soot buildup)
egr_exhaust_gas_recirculation.egr_cooler: Coolant-cooled heat exchanger; reduces EGR temp 1000°F+ to 300-400°F; failure = coolant in intake (white smoke, overheating, coolant loss)
egr_exhaust_gas_recirculation.problems: Soot buildup clogs intake, EGR valve, cooler; carbon in intake manifold; reduced power; cleaning or replacement needed; EGR delete illegal but common

dpf_diesel_particulate_filter.function: Traps soot particulates; ceramic honeycomb filter; captures 95%+ soot; required since 2007

regeneration.passive_regen: Occurs during highway driving; high exhaust temp (600°F+) burns off soot naturally; no driver intervention
regeneration.active_regen: ECM initiates; injects fuel into exhaust to raise temp to 1000-1200°F; burns soot; takes 20-40 min; requires driving (not parked); DPF light may illuminate
regeneration.parked_regen: Forced regen using scan tool; truck stationary; used when DPF too full for active regen; requires high idle, parking brake set; monitor exhaust temp
regeneration.cleaning: If regens don't work, DPF needs cleaning (baked in oven to burn ash) or replacement; ash from oil additives doesn't burn, accumulates over time

dpf_diesel_particulate_filter.soot_load: Measured in grams or %; 0-100% typical; regen triggers at 80-100%; if >150%, cleaning needed; scan tool reads soot load
dpf_diesel_particulate_filter.differential_pressure: Delta-P sensor measures pressure drop across DPF; high delta-P = clogged filter; >30+ inches H2O at highway speed = restricted
dpf_diesel_particulate_filter.problems: Clogged DPF (short trips, idling, failed regens), cracked substrate (overheat), sensor failures (delta-P, temp sensors)

scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.function: Reduces NOx by 90%+; injects DEF (Diesel Exhaust Fluid) into exhaust; DEF decomposes to ammonia, reacts with NOx over catalyst, produces N2 + H2O
scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.def_diesel_exhaust_fluid: 32.5% urea, 67.5% deionized water; freezes at 12°F (heater thaws); consumption 2-3% of fuel; must use DEF, not water or additives; stored in separate tank
scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.dosing_system: DEF pump, injector, lines; ECM controls dosing based on NOx sensors; dosing valve sprays DEF into decomposition tube
scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.nox_sensors: Upstream (pre-SCR) and downstream (post-SCR); monitors NOx levels and SCR efficiency; replacement $200-500 each
scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.scr_catalyst: Honeycomb structure coated with zeolite; 50,000-150,000 mile life typically; can be damaged by contaminated DEF or overheating
scr_selective_catalytic_reduction.problems: DEF contamination (diesel in DEF tank = entire system replacement $8,000+), crystallization (DEF solidifies in lines/injector), heater failures (cold climates), pump/injector failures, NOx sensor failures

doc_diesel_oxidation_catalyst.function: Oxidizes CO and hydrocarbons; converts to CO2 and H2O; first stage before DPF/SCR; helps with regen by creating heat
doc_diesel_oxidation_catalyst.location: Upstream of DPF; sometimes integrated into DPF housing
doc_diesel_oxidation_catalyst.failures: Rare; can be fouled by oil consumption or coolant contamination; coating can degrade with age/mileage

emissions_codes_derate.mil_malfunction_indicator: Check engine light; emissions fault detected; may or may not cause derate
emissions_codes_derate.amber_warning: First level; emissions system fault; no derate immediately; may escalate if not repaired
emissions_codes_derate.red_stop_lamp: Severe fault; derate imminent or active; requires immediate service

derate_levels.5_mph_derate: Severe; crawl speed only; must repair before operating; DEF system fault, SCR efficiency low
derate_levels.reduced_power: 25-75% power reduction; can still drive but limited; DPF full, NOx sensor faults
derate_levels.torque_limit: Limits torque output; gradual reduction; encourages service soon

emissions_codes_derate.inducement: EPA requires derate if emissions fault >100 miles or >100 engine hours; forces repair

system_components.compressor: Single or dual cylinder; belt or gear driven; governor cuts out at 120-135 PSI, cuts in at 100-105 PSI; pumps 10-14 CFM
system_components.air_dryer: Removes moisture and oil; desiccant cartridge; purges when compressor cuts out; replace desiccant every 3 years or per schedule
system_components.reservoirs: Wet tank (first, collects moisture), primary (rear brakes), secondary (front/steer brakes); drain daily in humid climates
system_components.brake_valves: Foot valve (service brakes), trailer hand valve, spring brake valve (parking/emergency), quick release valves, relay valves
system_components.brake_chambers: Type 20, 24, 30 (size); spring brakes on drive/trailer axles (spring applies, air releases); service chambers on all axles

air_system_tests.build_time_test: Pump down to 85 PSI, record time to build to 100 PSI; should be <2 min (25 PSI in <2 min); slow build = worn compressor, leak, governor issue
air_system_tests.leakage_test: Build to governor cutout; shut off engine; apply service brakes fully; should lose <3 PSI/min (single) or <4 PSI/min (combination); more = leak
air_system_tests.applied_leak_test: Build to max, shut off engine, release parking brake, apply service brake; <3 PSI/min single, <4 combination
air_system_tests.low_air_warning: Should activate at 60 PSI or above; buzzer and red light; test by pumping brakes with engine off
air_system_tests.spring_brake_activation: Should apply automatically at 20-40 PSI; parking brake pops out; emergency failsafe

troubleshooting.slow_air_build: Worn compressor valves/rings (rebuild kit $100-300), air leak (check all lines, fittings, valves with soapy water), governor not cutting in (adjust or replace), restricted intake/discharge lines
troubleshooting.excessive_purging: Air dryer purging constantly or very frequently; wet tank drain valve leaking (purge valve thinks system is at cutout), compressor discharge valve leaking, governor malfunction
troubleshooting.brakes_not_releasing: Insufficient air pressure (leak, compressor weak), spring brake not releasing (valve stuck, air leak in spring chamber line), frozen brake chamber (moisture in system, bad air dryer)
troubleshooting.brakes_dragging: Service chamber not venting (stuck valve, kinked hose), slack adjuster over-adjusted (1-2 inch stroke max), S-cam bushing worn (binds)
troubleshooting.uneven_braking: Out-of-adjustment (automatic slack adjusters stuck, manual not adjusted), contaminated linings (oil/grease), mismatched chambers, glad hand swapped (red/blue backwards)

abs_antilock_brakes.components: Wheel speed sensors (tone ring and sensor), ECU, modulator valves (holds/releases brake pressure)
abs_antilock_brakes.operation: Monitors wheel speed; if wheel decelerates too fast (locking), releases brake pressure briefly; cycles to maintain wheel rotation just before lockup
abs_antilock_brakes.indicator: ABS lamp on dash; illuminates if fault; amber light; truck still has brakes (ABS disabled, normal brakes work)
abs_antilock_brakes.troubleshooting: Check ABS light codes with scan tool; common faults: wheel speed sensor (damaged tone ring, sensor gap too large >0.050", wiring damage), modulator valve stuck, ECU failure, voltage issues

starting_charging.batteries: 12V in series = 24V typical (some 12V systems); group 31 common (1000+ CCA); load test 1/2 CCA for 15 sec, should maintain >9.6V per battery
starting_charging.cranking_amperage: Starter draws 300-800A; voltage should stay >9.6V (12V system) or >19V (24V system) while cranking; low voltage = weak battery, bad connection, starter issue
starting_charging.alternator_output: 24V system charges at 27.5-28.5V; 12V at 13.8-14.5V; load test: turn on loads (lights, fan), voltage should stay in range; below = alternator weak or regulator fault
starting_charging.voltage_drop_testing: Starter circuit: <0.5V drop from battery positive to starter positive during crank; <0.5V ground side; more = bad cable or connection; replace or clean

j1939_can_bus.function: Multiplexed network; ECM, transmission, ABS, instrument cluster communicate; two-wire twisted pair (CAN-H, CAN-L); 250k or 500k baud
j1939_can_bus.terminating_resistors: 120 ohm resistors at each end of network; measure between CAN-H and CAN-L with network unpowered: should read 60 ohms (two 120 in parallel)
j1939_can_bus.troubleshooting: Scan tool reads J1939 data; if no communication, check terminating resistors, wiring shorts/opens, damaged modules; disconnect modules one at a time to isolate bad module pulling bus down
j1939_can_bus.dtcs: Diagnostic Trouble Codes; SPN (Suspect Parameter Number, identifies sensor/component), FMI (Failure Mode Identifier, type of fault 0-31); example SPN 94 FMI 3 = Fuel delivery pressure voltage high

sensors_inputs.thermistors: NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient); coolant temp, intake air temp, oil temp; resistance decreases as temp increases; 2000-3000 ohms at 70°F typical; test with ohmmeter and compare to chart
sensors_inputs.map_boost_pressure: Manifold Absolute Pressure or boost sensor; 5V reference, signal voltage varies with pressure; 0.5V at atmospheric, 4.5V at full boost; test with vacuum/pressure pump and scan tool
sensors_inputs.tps_throttle_position: Potentiometer; 5V reference; 0.5-1V closed throttle, 4-4.5V wide open; modern use pedal position sensors (two sensors for redundancy, compare voltages)
sensors_inputs.crankshaft_camshaft_position: Magnetic or Hall-effect; generate signal as reluctor wheel passes; waveform should be clean; weak signal = no start; test with scope or signal voltage with meter

common_electrical_faults.no_crank: Check battery voltage >12.4V per battery; listen for starter solenoid click (if no click = open circuit to solenoid, bad ignition switch/neutral safety); if click but no crank = bad starter, poor ground, seized engine
common_electrical_faults.crank_no_start: Check fuel pressure (rail pressure 5,000+ PSI cranking for common rail), check for codes, verify crankshaft/camshaft sensors (need both for sync), check for air in fuel
common_electrical_faults.intermittent_issues: Wiggle test connectors and harnesses; check for chafed wires, corroded terminals; use scan tool freeze frame to see conditions when fault occurred
common_electrical_faults.parasitic_draw: Disconnect batteries negative; put ammeter in series; should be <50 mA (0.050A); more = something staying on; pull fuses one at a time to isolate circuit

clutch.types: Single plate (light duty), dual plate (heavy duty), angle spring, perpendicular spring; self-adjusting most common now
clutch.clutch_brake: Small friction disc on input shaft; depressed last 1-2 inches of pedal travel; stops input shaft for gear engagement when stopped
clutch.free_play: 1.5-2 inches pedal free travel before resistance; too little = clutch slipping, too much = clutch not releasing; adjust at linkage or self-adjusting mechanism

failure_modes.slipping: Engine RPM rises but road speed doesn't; smell of burning friction material; caused by worn disc, oil contamination, insufficient free play (riding clutch)
failure_modes.not_releasing: Hard to shift, grinding gears; excessive free play, air in hydraulic system, warped disc, bad release bearing
failure_modes.chatter: Vibration during engagement; oil on disc, worn springs, loose engine mounts, flywheel damaged

manual.types: Synchronized (Eaton Fuller 10-speed typical), non-synchronized (older, double-clutch required), automated manual (UltraShift, Volvo I-Shift)
manual.splitter: High/low range in same gear position; doubles gear count; 10-speed = 5 positions × 2 (range)
manual.shift_problems: Hard shifting = linkage adjustment, clutch not releasing, synchronizer worn; jumping out of gear = worn shift fork, detent spring weak, gear/slider worn; grinding = clutch issue, synchronizer bad

automatic_allison.models: 1000/2000 series light/medium duty, 3000/4000 heavy duty; 5-6 speeds; torque converter; planetary gearsets
automatic_allison.fluid: TranSynd or Dexron; check hot in neutral; level critical (overfull = foaming, aeration; underfull = slipping, overheating)
automatic_allison.prognostics: Built-in monitoring; scan tool reads clutch wear, shift adapt values, temp history; predicts maintenance needs
automatic_allison.common_issues: Slipping (low fluid, worn clutches, valve body issue), no movement (low fluid, pump failure, torque converter failed), harsh shifts (dirty fluid, valve body, solenoid fault)

differentials.types: Single reduction, double reduction (higher torque capacity), tandem (forward/rear axles)
differentials.gear_ratios: 3.70, 4.10, 4.30 typical; higher number = more torque, lower speed; lower number = highway gearing
differentials.limited_slip_locking: No-Spin (automatic locker), air-actuated diff lock, limited-slip clutches; prevents one wheel spin
differentials.oil_analysis: Check for metal particles (gear wear), check level (weeping seals common), fluid type (synthetic vs conventional, GL-5 rating, 75W-90 or 80W-140)
differentials.failures: Gear whine (worn gears, bearing preload incorrect), clunking (backlash excessive, worn spider gears), leaks (pinion seal, axle seals, cover gasket)

driveshaft_u_joints.driveshaft_angles: Operating angle <3° ideal, <5° acceptable; excessive angle = vibration, rapid u-joint wear; check with angle finder
driveshaft_u_joints.u_joint_diagnosis: Clunk on acceleration/deceleration = worn u-joint or slip yoke; vibration = out of balance, bad u-joint, phasing incorrect; check for play by rotating shaft and watching joint
driveshaft_u_joints.replacement: Mark yokes before removal (phasing critical); press out old caps, press in new, ensure clips seat; grease fittings, pump until grease comes out all four bearing caps
driveshaft_u_joints.slip_yoke: Splined connection allows driveshaft length change (suspension movement); must be lubricated; insufficient spline engagement = vibration, failure

cooling_system.capacity: 20-40 gallons typical Class 8 truck; 50/50 ethylene glycol and water; testing: refractometer or test strips (not hydrometer like batteries)
cooling_system.sca_supplemental_coolant_additive: Required for heavy duty (Detroit Diesel, Cummins); prevents cylinder liner pitting (cavitation erosion); test with strip, add if low; 3-6% concentration
cooling_system.thermostats: 180-195°F typical opening temp; some dual thermostats; stuck closed = overheat; stuck open = slow warm-up, poor heater, emissions issues (DPF won't regen)
cooling_system.water_pump: Belt or gear-driven; leaks from weep hole (bearing failure warning); cavitation damage to impeller from poor coolant maintenance
cooling_system.failures: Overheating (low coolant, air in system, failed thermostat, plugged radiator, fan clutch not engaging, blown head gasket), external leaks (hoses, gaskets), internal leaks (EGR cooler, head gasket = coolant in oil or exhaust)

fuel_system.fuel_supply: Lift pump draws fuel from tank, pushes to high-pressure pump (common rail) or injection pump; pressure 10-80 PSI typical at lift pump
fuel_system.fuel_filters: Primary (water separator with drain), secondary (finer filtration 2-10 micron); change per interval (contamination = injector failure)
fuel_system.air_in_fuel: Causes hard start, loss of power, rough idle, stalling; find leak on suction side (loose fittings, cracked lines, bad O-rings); prime system after filter change or repair
fuel_system.water_separator: Collects water from fuel; must drain regularly; some have sensor (water in fuel light); water causes rust and injector failure
fuel_system.common_rail_pressure: Cranking 5,000-10,000 PSI; running 20,000-30,000 PSI; low pressure = hard start, loss power; codes for rail pressure low; check high-pressure pump, pressure relief valve, injector leaks
fuel_system.return_fuel: Excess fuel from injectors returns to tank; excessive return = injector leaking (perform return flow test, isolate bad injector); also cools injectors

systematic_approach.verify_complaint: Talk to driver; reproduce issue if possible; note conditions when it occurs
systematic_approach.pull_codes: Use scan tool; note active and inactive codes; freeze frame data shows conditions when code set
systematic_approach.research: Look up codes in service manual or online; understand what system is affected; check TSBs (Technical Service Bulletins)
systematic_approach.test: Follow diagnostic tree; test components per manual; use multimeter, pressure gauges, scope as needed
systematic_approach.fix: Repair or replace faulty component; clear codes; road test; verify repair
systematic_approach.document: Record what was found, parts used, tests performed; helps with comebacks

tools.scan_tool: Required; reads codes, live data, performs tests (injector cutout, actuator tests); OEM software best (Cummins Insite, Detroit DDDL, Cat ET); generic tools limited
tools.multimeter: Voltage, resistance, continuity, amperage; Fluke or similar quality; most used diagnostic tool
tools.pressure_gauges: Fuel pressure, boost pressure, oil pressure, coolant pressure testing; specific to task
tools.oscilloscope: Waveform analysis; sensor outputs, injector drivers, CAN bus; advanced diagnostics; identifies intermittent issues
tools.infrared_thermometer: Non-contact temp; check cylinder temps (misfire = cooler), bearing temps, exhaust temps

common_driveability_issues.hard_start: Check fuel pressure, air in fuel, glow plugs (cold weather), crank/cam sensors, ECM power/grounds, battery voltage during crank
common_driveability_issues.loss_of_power: Check boost pressure (turbo, intercooler leak, intake leak), fuel pressure (pump, filter, injectors), EGR stuck open, DPF plugged, transmission not downshifting, pyrometer (exhaust temp high = restriction)
common_driveability_issues.rough_idle: Injector fault (perform contribution test, cutout test), air in fuel, low fuel pressure, EGR valve stuck, engine mounts broken
common_driveability_issues.black_smoke: Overfueling = injector leaking, turbo not boosting (leak, wastegate stuck, VGT fault), intake restriction (filter, EGR buildup), timing incorrect
common_driveability_issues.white_smoke: Coolant in combustion (head gasket, EGR cooler, cracked head); unburned fuel (low compression, bad injector, cold engine - normal on startup)
common_driveability_issues.blue_smoke: Oil consumption = worn rings, valve seals, turbo seals; check oil level, pull intake tube (oil in intake = turbo seals or EGR)
